que: What is `lateinit` mainly used for in Kotlin?
options: Deferring initialization of a non-null mutable property

que: Which kind of property can use `lateinit`?
Which type is not allowed with `lateinit`?
What happens if a `lateinit` property is accessed before initialization?
options: It returns a default value automatically
Computes a value on first access and caches it
Recomputes the value on every access

que: Which property style is commonly paired with `lazy`?
Which is a good use case for `lateinit`?
options: A dependency initialized in test setup or lifecycle code
A value that should never change

que: Which is a good use case for `lazy`?
options: Deferring expensive one-time setup until first use

que: What is Kotlin's default behavior for `lazy` initialization regarding threads?
options: It always runs on the main thread
It is recalculated by every thread

que: What is a common mistake with deferred initialization?
options: Using `lateinit` when a read-only `lazy` property would express the intent better
